public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as toll witness.
manner: Thinks in testimony, signatures, and remembered sequence, even when everyone else wants to move faster. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a toll witness still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of toll witness in and around Amber Tides River, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Amber Tides River workable through the obligations of toll witness while managing the immediate pressure of resource depletion in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must preserve sequence and custody even when powerful parties would prefer ambiguity. Around Amber Tides River, that usually means carrying resource depletion, sabotage, predatory frontier ecology without letting the role of toll witness visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Lysa March needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Amber Tides River.
Lysa March can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Lysa March communicates in public.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Amber Tides River: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
